skill.name: word-stats
skill.description: Get word count, character count, reading time, and text statistics. Quick analysis without questions.
skill.summary: Get word count, character count, reading time, and text statistics. Quick analysis without questions...
skill.author: humanizerai

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and dismissed as false positives. The weak cryptography alerts point to natural-language text, and the external command alert points to a fenced output template, not executable code. No prompt injection, network access, filesystem access, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3, but the line is the skill description for word, character, reading time, and text statistics. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, encryption API, or security-sensitive code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is plain frontmatter text describing character counts. There is no code execution or cryptographic operation to evaluate.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 14, but the line only says the user provides text in $ARGUMENTS. No weak cryptographic algorithm or credential-handling behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is an input description for text analysis. It contains no crypto library names, algorithm names, or executable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby or Shell Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 20, but the backticks begin a Markdown fenced output example. The skill contains no Ruby code, shell command, subprocess call, or instruction to execute user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is Markdown formatting for a display template. Surrounding lines show tables with placeholder metrics, not commands.

content.user_title: Analyze Text Length and Reading Time
content.value_statement: Writers often need quick text metrics without setting up a separate tool. This skill returns word counts, character counts, reading time, speaking time, and sentence-level statistics in a compact table.
actual_capabilities: Counts words, characters with spaces, and characters without spaces.
Estimates reading time using 238 words per minute.
Estimates speaking time using 150 words per minute.
Calculates unique word percentage and average word length.
Identifies the longest word and sentence length range.
limitations: Accuracy depends on how the assistant tokenizes punctuation, abbreviations, and unusual formatting.
Reading and speaking times are estimates based on fixed average speeds.
It does not edit, summarize, or improve the text unless the user asks.
It is designed for text pasted in the prompt, not document files.

use_cases.title: Check article length
use_cases.description: Get word count and reading time before publishing a blog post, article, or newsletter.

use_cases.title: Prepare spoken remarks
use_cases.description: Estimate speaking time for a speech, script, presentation, or recorded message.

use_cases.title: Review assignment limits
use_cases.description: Measure words, sentences, and paragraphs against assignment or submission requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Basic word count
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you only need quick metrics for pasted text.
prompt_templates.prompt: Count the words, characters, sentences, paragraphs, reading time, and speaking time in this text: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Publication check
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before publishing a post or newsletter.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this draft and report the standard word statistics, including reading time and unique word percentage: [paste draft]

prompt_templates.title: Speech timing
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when preparing remarks for a fixed time slot.
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate the speaking time, word count, sentence count, and longest sentence for this script: [paste script]

prompt_templates.title: Detailed text audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when comparing readability-related structure across a long passage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full word statistics report for this text. Include counts, time estimates, word diversity, average sentence length, and sentence length range: [paste text]

output_examples.input: A short paragraph for a newsletter introduction.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a compact report with word count, character counts, sentence count, paragraph count, reading time, speaking time, unique words, average word length, and sentence length metrics.
The skill reports about 6 minutes of speaking time, about 4 minutes of reading time, and supporting counts for words, characters, sentences, and paragraphs.

output_examples.input: Several paragraphs from a student essay.
output_examples.output: The skill shows whether the essay is near the required word count and gives sentence and paragraph totals for structure review.

best_practices: Paste the exact final text when you need publication-ready counts.
Use plain text when possible to avoid counting hidden formatting or markup.
Ask for only statistics when you do not want editing suggestions.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as the only authority for legal, academic, or platform submission limits.
Do not expect exact timing for speakers with very fast or slow delivery.
Do not provide confidential text unless your AI tool and workspace are approved for that data.

faq.question: Does this skill edit my writing?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on text statistics and avoids recommendations unless you ask for them.

faq.question: How is reading time calculated?
faq.answer: It divides the word count by 238 words per minute.

faq.question: How is speaking time calculated?
faq.answer: It divides the word count by 150 words per minute.

faq.question: Can it count characters without spaces?
faq.answer: Yes. It reports characters with spaces and characters without spaces.

faq.question: Does it work with long pasted text?
faq.answer: Yes, within the context limits of the AI tool you use.

faq.question: Can it analyze files directly?
faq.answer: The skill is written for pasted text in the prompt. File handling depends on the host tool.
